description: Hand-authored capability floor per task type (#350/#363): which models clear the bar for each task-type label emitted by the auto-router classifier. Consulted by auto-router's resolveByTaskType since #352 (ADR-0078) when matrix routing is enabled (the default since #353/ADR-0079; /auto matrix off opts out). Closed-world for matrix picks: a model absent here is never a matrix pick, though the classifier may still choose it — so absence never removes a model from routing. Ranking happens only WITHIN a capable set: local providers rank first when local use is allowed, then cost-ranking (input + k*output, k=1), then context window and provider/id tie-breaks. Task types are the closed taxonomy in auto-router/types.ts TASK_TYPES. Human-reviewed, never auto-written by routing; refresh/review is user-invoked and auditable (mirrors the #344 cost-table posture): update capable sets only with a rationale and a fresh lastReviewed.

omlx/coding-workhorse.rationale: GLM-4.7-Flash-6bit, the sole pinned oMLX resident (local-llm ADR-009/010): tool-call-strong, text-only, designed for read-only subagent fan-out (reads/edits, search, codegen, summarization, tool-calling) — hence agentic-loop and the code types. NOT long-context despite the 202K native window: sustained concurrency (mark of 8, ADR-010) is prefill-activation-bound and falls as context grows; the sustained mark was validated at ~16K-token prefixes, and the pi registration deliberately caps contextWindow at 131072 — long-context work goes to the frontier. NOT creative: quality-tier work is deliberately not served locally (ADR-009).
